arts.ttl: Virgil van Dijk slams Liverpool's 'reality check' defeat to Nottingham Forest
arts.sum: "Overall it was a disappointing afternoon, something we didn't want to happen but we have to turn this around and make this a sort of reality check to be better for the rest of the season because if you want to achieve things this season the contrast between the games we played is too big." "Obviously everyone is disappointed, like we all should be, but we also remind ourselves that Tuesday is another big game," Van Dijk stressed.

arts.ttl: 'I'm an Arsenal icon – this is what I think of Liverpool's title chances after Forest loss'
arts.sum: Arne Slot's Liverpool reign hit a snag when he saw his side bested 1-0 at home by assumed strugglers Nottingham Forest, but a former Arsenal star believes their title hopes weren't dented

arts.ttl: Football transfer rumours: Haaland wants Man City exit; Man Utd & Liverpool eye Adeyemi
arts.sum: Bayern Munich are ready to step up contract talks with Man City target Jamal Musiala, despite admitting it will be difficult to reach an agreement with the 21-year-old. Borussia Dortmund are ready to cash in on forward Karim Adeyemi, with Liverpool, Man Utd and Newcastle United all prepared to make big offers for the Germany international.

arts.ttl: Ex-Liverpool defender Joel Matip 'receives offers' from three Premier League clubs
arts.sum: Former Liverpool defender Joel Matip reportedly receives as many as three offers from Premier League clubs looking to take advantage of his status as a free agent.

arts.ttl: Sacchi: ‘Vlahovic will have more help against PSV, Leao needs to be more switched on against Liverpool
arts.sum: Former Milan and Italy coach Arrigo Sacchi believes an Italian side could win the Champions League this season. Speaking to La Gazzetta dello Sport, Sacchi previewed all five Serie A sides in the competition, explaining what he expects from each match: Juventus-PSV Tuesday, 18:45 Dutch teams are generally difficult to face. We must not forget that they have the sense of play and collective in their DNA, being the first to develop modern football. PSV will give Juventus a hard time. In the league, the Dutch have won five out of five matches, they’re going very strong. arts.ttl: Virgil van Dijk explains Champions League motivation and fires warning to Liverpool stars
arts.sum: Liverpool return to the Champions League at AC Milan on Tuesday off the back of a shock defeat at home to Nottingham Forest at the weekend
